Step-1 Load Dataset into SQL Database¶

Step-2 Explore Table(schema,sample data)¶

2. describe sample - superstore;¶

Insight:¶

The schema provides information about column names and data types,which is essential for further analysis¶

2. select * from sample - superstore limit 10;¶

Insight:¶

The query displays sample records from the dataset and helps understand the structure and available attributes.¶

Step-3 Apply where filters¶

1. Region¶

select Order ID,Sales,Category,Region from sample - superstore where Region='West';¶

Insight:¶

The query retrieves sales records belonging only to the West region for regional analysis.¶

2. Category¶

select Order ID,Sales,Category from sample - superstore where Category='Technology';¶

Insight:¶

The query focuses on products in the Technology category to analyze category-specific sales.¶

3. Date¶

select Order ID,Order Date,Region,Sales from sample - superstore where str_to_date(Order Date,'%m/%d/%Y') between '2016-01-01' and '2016-12-31';¶

Insight:¶

The query filters records within a specified date range to analyze time-based sales activity.¶

4. Sales¶

select Order ID,Sales from sample - superstore where Sales>500;¶

Insight:¶

The query identifies high-value transactions with sales greater than 500.¶

Step-4 GROUP BY for aggregations¶

1. Sales¶

select Category,SUM(Sales) from sample - superstore group by Category;¶

Insight:¶

The query summarizes total sales for each category, enabling category-wise performance comparison.¶

2.Averages¶

select Category,AVG(sales) from sample - superstore group by Category;¶

Insight:¶

The query calculates average sales per category to evaluate typical transaction values¶

3. Quantity¶

select Category,AVG(Quantity) from sample - superstore group by Category;¶

Insight:¶

The query measures the total quantity sold in each category.¶

Step-5 Sort and limit results¶

Top Categories¶

select Category,SUM(Sales) as Total_Sales from sample - superstore group by Category order by Total_Sales desc limit 3;¶

Insight:¶

The query ranks categories based on total sales contribution.¶

Top Products¶

select Product Name,SUM(Sales) as Total_Sales from sample - superstore group by Product Name order by Total_Sales desc limit 5;¶

Insight:¶

The query identifies the highest revenue-generating products in the dataset.¶

Step-6 Solve use cases¶

1. Monthly Trend¶

select year(str_to_date(Order Date,'%m/%d/%Y')) as year_,month(str_to_date(Order Date,'%m/%d/%Y')) as month_, sum(sales) as total_sales from sample - superstore group by year_,month_ order by year_,month_;¶

Insight:¶

The query analyzes sales trends over time and helps identify monthly variations in revenue.¶

2. Top 10 customers¶

select Customer Name,sum(sales) as total_sales from sample - superstore group by Customer Name order by total_sales desc limit 10;¶

Insight:¶

The query identifies customers who contributed the most revenue.¶

3. Duplicate orders¶

select Order ID,count() as duplicate_count from sample - superstore group by Order ID having count()>1;¶

Insight:¶

The query checks for repeated Order IDs and helps assess data consistency.¶

Step-7 Validate results¶

1. Row count¶

select count(*) as Total_Rows from sample - superstore;¶

Insight:¶

The query verifies the total number of records available for analysis.¶

Data Quality¶

a. Check Missing Sales Values¶

select count(*) as missing_sales from sample - superstore where sales is null;¶

Insight:¶

The query checks for missing values in Sales to ensure data quality.¶

b. Check Missing Customer Names¶

select count(*) as missing_customers from sample - superstore where Customer Name is null;¶

Insight:¶

The query checks for missing values in Customers to ensure data quality.¶

c. Check Missing Product Names¶

select count(*) as missing_products from sample - superstore where Product Name is null;¶

Insight:¶

The query checks for missing values in Products to ensure data quality.¶
